name: Acute Respiratory Infection (ARI)
cause: Viral or bacterial infection of upper/lower respiratory tract. Transmitted via respiratory droplets.
symptoms: Cough, fever, sore throat, runny/blocked nose, shortness of breath, chest pain.
description: Common infectious disease affecting the respiratory system. Severity ranges from mild URI to severe pneumonia. Most cases self-limit within 1-2 weeks.
additional_info: Supportive care: fluids, rest, symptomatic treatment. Antibiotics only if bacterial. Vaccination prevents some strains.

name: Malaria
cause: Plasmodium parasites (P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae, P. knowlesi) transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes.
symptoms: Fever (often high, cyclic), chills, sweats, headache, muscle aches, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea.
description: Serious parasitic infection endemic in tropical/subtropical regions. Can be life-threatening if untreated, especially P. falciparum. Symptoms typically appear 7-30 days after infection.
additional_info: Diagnosis: blood smear microscopy, rapid diagnostic tests, PCR. Treatment depends on species, severity, and drug resistance. Prophylaxis for travelers.

name: Tuberculosis (TB)
cause: Mycobacterium tuberculosis transmitted via airborne droplets. Can be latent or active.
symptoms: Persistent cough (>3 weeks), hemoptysis, chest pain, fever, night sweats, weight loss, fatigue.
description: Chronic bacterial infection primarily affecting lungs. Can progress to severe illness if untreated. Major global health concern. Latent TB: no symptoms but can reactivate.
additional_info: Diagnosis: chest X-ray, sputum smear microscopy, TB culture, TB-IGRA. Treatment: 6-month multi-drug regimen. Highly curable if treated early.

name: Typhoid Fever
cause: Salmonella typhi bacteria, transmitted via contaminated food/water.
symptoms: High sustained fever, headache, muscle aches, weakness, abdominal pain, rose spots rash, delirium.
description: Systemic bacterial infection causing prolonged fever. Common in areas with poor sanitation. Can progress to severe complications (perforation, shock) without treatment.
additional_info: Diagnosis: blood culture, Widal test. Treatment: antibiotics (fluoroquinolones, cephalosporins). Prevention: typhoid vaccination, safe water.

name: Cholera
cause: Vibrio cholerae bacteria, transmitted via contaminated water.
symptoms: Sudden onset severe watery diarrhea (rice-water stool), vomiting, dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, hypotension.
description: Acute infectious disease causing severe diarrhea and rapid dehydration. Fatality rate high without prompt treatment (up to 60%). Rapid onset (hours to days).
additional_info: Treatment: aggressive fluid and electrolyte replacement (oral rehydration, IV fluids). Antibiotics shorten duration. Vaccination available. Prevention: safe water, sanitation.

name: Dengue Fever
cause: Dengue virus (DENV-1-4) transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes, primarily in tropical regions.
symptoms: Sudden fever, severe headache, joint/muscle pain, rash, bleeding (in severe cases), shock.
description: Common vector-borne viral disease. Incubation 3-14 days. Most recover within a week, but dengue hemorrhagic fever/shock syndrome can be life-threatening.
additional_info: Diagnosis: serology (IgM/IgG), RT-PCR, NS1 antigen. No specific antiviral. Treatment: supportive care, fluid management. Dengue vaccine available in some countries.

name: Diabetes Mellitus Type 2
cause: Insulin resistance, relative insulin deficiency. Genetics, obesity, sedentary lifestyle, age.
symptoms: Polyuria, polydipsia, fatigue, blurred vision, slow wound healing, numbness/tingling (neuropathy).
description: Chronic metabolic disorder affecting blood glucose regulation. Most common type (~90% of diabetes cases). Often asymptomatic in early stages, discovered incidentally.
additional_info: Diagnosis: fasting glucose, HbA1c, oral glucose tolerance test. Management: lifestyle (diet, exercise), oral medications, insulin. Complications: cardiovascular, neuropathy, retinopathy, nephropathy.

name: Hypertension
cause: Multifactorial: genetics, salt intake, obesity, alcohol, stress, secondary causes (renal, endocrine).
symptoms: Often asymptomatic. Severe cases: headache, dizziness, shortness of breath, chest pain.
description: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure (≥140/90 mmHg). Major risk factor for stroke, MI, kidney disease. Diagnosed after repeated measurements.
additional_info: Management: lifestyle (DASH diet, exercise, reduce salt/alcohol), antihypertensive medications. Regular monitoring essential. Target BP <130/80 for high-risk patients.

name: Pneumonia
cause: Bacterial (S. pneumoniae, H. influenzae, S. aureus), viral (influenza, RSV), or fungal pathogens. Community or hospital-acquired.
symptoms: Cough, fever, dyspnea, chest pain, sputum production (purulent, blood-tinged), crackles on auscultation.
description: Acute infection of lung parenchyma causing alveolar consolidation. Can range from mild (walking pneumonia) to severe sepsis. Higher mortality in elderly/immunocompromised.
additional_info: Diagnosis: chest X-ray, sputum culture, blood culture. Treatment: antibiotics (empiric, then targeted), supportive care, oxygen if needed. Prevention: pneumococcal vaccine.

name: Influenza (Flu)
cause: Influenza viruses (A, B, C, D). Spread via respiratory droplets. High transmissibility, seasonal epidemics.
symptoms: Abrupt onset fever, cough, sore throat, myalgias, fatigue, headache. GI symptoms in children.
description: Acute viral respiratory infection. Incubation 1-4 days. Most recover within 1 week, but high-risk groups prone to severe disease/complications (pneumonia, myocarditis).
additional_info: Diagnosis: rapid antigen test, RT-PCR. Treatment: oseltamivir (Tamiflu) if started early (<48h). Annual vaccination. Supportive care essential.

name: HIV/AIDS
cause: Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) transmission via sexual contact, blood, mother-to-child.
symptoms: Acute infection: fever, rash, lymphadenopathy. Chronic: asymptomatic until CD4 <200 (AIDS). AIDS-defining opportunistic infections.
description: Chronic retroviral infection destroying CD4+ T cells. Untreated: progressive immunosuppression leading to AIDS and death. With ART: near-normal life expectancy.
additional_info: Diagnosis: HIV antibody test, HIV RNA (viral load), CD4 count. Treatment: antiretroviral therapy (ART) first-line. Prevention: condoms, PrEP, safe practices. No cure, but manageable.

name: Hepatitis B
cause: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) transmission via blood, sexual contact, mother-to-child, contaminated needles.
symptoms: Acute: jaundice, fatigue, abdominal pain, dark urine. Chronic: often asymptomatic until cirrhosis.
description: Viral infection of the liver. Acute infection resolves in 90% of adults; chronic in ~10%. Chronic HBV: risk of cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma.
additional_info: Diagnosis: HBsAg, anti-HBc, HBV DNA. Treatment: antivirals (entecavir, tenofovir) if chronic with high viral load/ALT. Prevention: vaccination (highly effective).

name: Asthma
cause: Genetic predisposition + environmental triggers (allergens, pollution, viral infection, exercise, stress).
symptoms: Wheezing, shortness of breath, cough (especially at night), chest tightness, exacerbations triggered by allergens/exercise.
description: Chronic inflammatory airway disease with reversible airflow obstruction. Affects children and adults. Severity ranges from intermittent to severe persistent.
additional_info: Diagnosis: spirometry, peak flow, bronchial challenge test. Treatment: controller (inhaled corticosteroids) + rescue inhalers (beta-2 agonists). Avoid triggers. Prognosis good with proper management.

name: Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis)
cause: Genetic predisposition, impaired skin barrier, dysregulated immune response, environmental triggers (irritants, allergens).
symptoms: Intense itching, erythema, dry skin, vesicles, crusting, lichenification (chronic). Often worse at night.
description: Chronic inflammatory skin condition. Usually onset in infancy/early childhood. Highly pruritic, can severely impact quality of life. Exacerbated by stress, weather changes.
additional_info: Diagnosis: clinical. Treatment: skin care (moisturizers), topical corticosteroids/calcineurin inhibitors, antihistamines. Severe: systemic immunosuppressants. Avoid triggers.

name: Obesity
cause: Excessive caloric intake, sedentary lifestyle, genetic factors, metabolic disorders, medications, psychological factors.
symptoms: Excess body fat, BMI ≥30, often asymptomatic. Associated: dyspnea, joint pain, sleep apnea, fatty liver.
description: Chronic condition characterized by excess body fat. Major risk factor for T2DM, hypertension, dyslipidemia, cardiovascular disease, certain cancers.
additional_info: Diagnosis: BMI, waist circumference, body composition. Management: lifestyle (calorie deficit, exercise), behavioral therapy, medications (rare), bariatric surgery (severe cases).

name: Depression
cause: Neurotransmitter dysregulation (serotonin, dopamine), genetic predisposition, life stressors, trauma, chronic illness.
symptoms: Persistent sadness, anhedonia, fatigue, sleep disturbance, appetite change, guilt, poor concentration, suicidal thoughts.
description: Common mental health disorder causing persistent depressed mood and loss of interest/pleasure. Can be mild to severe. High comorbidity with anxiety, substance abuse.
additional_info: Diagnosis: clinical interview, symptom severity scales. Treatment: psychotherapy (CBT, IPT), SSRIs, SNRIs, tricyclic antidepressants. ECT for severe/refractory cases. Prognosis good with treatment.
